reasoning:
The baseline data point (for ~2025-03-30) will likely be around 223k, following a rapid two-week decline from a local peak of 228k. 
- For the short term (April 6 and April 29), momentum is clearly downward toward the 215k baseline seen earlier this year, making an increase relative to 223k unlikely (estimated at 33% and 40%).
- For the medium term (June 28), initial claims often see a localized spike in the summer due to auto plant retoolings and educational sector shifts, which seasonal adjustment sometimes under-corrects (similar bumps happened in summer 2023 and 2024). Thus, the probability shifts to slightly above 50% (56%).
- For longer term horizons (Sept 26 and March 2026), the forecast grows increasingly uncertain, collapsing towards 0.5. Since 223k is historically quite low and labor force growth along with asymmetric economic downside risks (recessions easily push claims to 300k+, while the structural floor is ~200k) skew the long-term outlook to the upside, probabilities remain marginally above 50% (53% and 51% respectively).
